messages.content:
I see that TicketEditor does some normalization when loading (lines 153-162), but this is on the frontend after the ticket has already been parsed. The issue is that the JSON file itself contains malformed data that fails Zod validation.

I have enough context now. Let me summarize what I've found:

Okay, I've found the root cause of this issue. Here's what's happening:

The ticket DB-0041 has malformed data in its JSON file. The acceptance_criteria and implementation_steps arrays contain objects that are missing the required "text" property. The Zod schema in schemas.ts (lines 26-40) expects either a plain string OR an object with {text: string, completed: boolean}, but the actual data seems to have objects with just {completed: false} or some other structure without "text".

When you press Start the first time, the readTicket function fails because the Zod validation is strict. On the second press, something must be different (maybe a different code path or cached data).

The fix would be to make the schema more resilient by adding data normalization or more lenient parsing, so malformed tickets don't block the agent launcher entirely.

One question before I finalize the requirements: Do you want the fix to just handle the error gracefully (show a warning but continue), OR do you want it to automatically repair/migrate the malformed ticket data when it's detected?

Got it, that makes sense. Automatically repairing the data is the cleaner solution.

Based on the codebase, the best place to add this repair logic would be in the TicketFileStore.readTicket method (around line 61-78 in ticketFileStore.ts). Before parsing with Zod, we can normalize the acceptance_criteria and implementation_steps arrays to ensure they have the correct structure. If an item is an object without a "text" property, we can either convert it to a default structure or skip it.

I have everything I need to write up this ticket.
wait dont you think it got that error because it wasnt able to read the ticket at all

Okay, I think I understand now. Looking at your logs again more carefully:

The FIRST client connection (nZKxLsgn7EaC2zOFAAAn) had ticketId as "undefined" (literally the string "undefined" or actually undefined). This first connection seemed to NOT have a proper ticket context. Then the SECOND connection (a57jUHIawlr9jrJCAAAp) had DB-0044 properly set.

But wait, looking at the log output again - the "Failed to read ticket DB-0041" error happened BEFORE any client connected. That error is from the board page loading (actions.ts line 476), trying to read all tickets to display the board.

So here's what I think happened:
1. Board page loads
2. getTickets tries to read ALL tickets including DB-0041
3. DB-0041 has malformed data, fails with ZodError
4. This failure might have caused some state issue with the board
5. First click on Start - something was not initialized properly, ticketId came through as undefined
6. Second click on Start - board had recovered, ticketId came through correctly as DB-0044

You're right that the Zod validation failure during board load might be cascading and causing the AgentLauncher to receive an undefined ticketId on the first click. The fix should happen at the data reading level so that malformed tickets don't crash the entire board load.

One more clarifying question: Do you want the fix to also show some kind of warning or notification when a ticket has been auto-repaired, so you know which tickets had bad data? Or should it just silently fix the data?

Ah I see, thanks for the additional logs! This confirms my earlier theory. Looking at the stack traces:

The error happens at the API route level (/api/board/tickets/route.js) when trying to load tickets for the board. The readTicket function in ticketFileStore fails because of the malformed acceptance_criteria and implementation_steps data.

What's interesting is I can see multiple client connections happening in sequence - the first connection (E2Sza-Q8HY89lr4RAAAr) had worktree mode DISABLED and shows as a fresh connection right after the ZodError. Then subsequent connections (nmNPPn2kVAuettF-AAAt and others) all show as "RESUME FLOW for ticket DB-0044" with worktree mode ENABLED.

So the behavior you're seeing - needing to press Start twice - is likely because:
1. First click: Board tries to load tickets, DB-0041 fails validation, something in the state gets messed up, first client connects but with incomplete/wrong state
2. Second click: Board has cached or recovered enough that the second connection works properly

For the silent auto-repair approach, I have enough information now. The fix will add data normalization in the readTicket function before Zod parsing to automatically fix malformed acceptance_criteria and implementation_steps entries.
